entities.name: Con Edison
entities.legal_role: Con Edison is contractually obligated to develop and maintain the chemical inventory and implement the Risk Management Plan upon approval, making it responsible for compliance with chemical reporting and safety implementation at the facility.
entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall develop and maintain an inventory of all chemicals and hazardous substances... Con Edison shall notify the Power Authority... Con Edison shall be responsible for the implementation of the RMP...

entities.entity_type: Contracting Party and Regulatory Compliance Obligor
entities.name: Power Authority
entities.legal_role: The Power Authority is responsible for preparing, submitting, and maintaining the Risk Management Plan, conducting inspections, obtaining and submitting Material Safety Data Sheets, appointing an Emergency Coordinator, and filing required hazardous substance disclosure forms.
entities.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall be responsible for: i. Preparing, submitting, and maintaining the Risk Management Plan... ii. Conducting periodic inspections... The Power Authority shall obtain and submit Material Safety Data Sheets... The Power Authority shall appoint an Emergency Coordinator... The Power Authority shall be responsible for the filing of all Facility Inventory Forms...

entities.name: Chemical Inventory
entities.legal_role: A legally mandated inventory of all chemicals and hazardous substances subject to reporting requirements under the Community Right To Know Law, which must be developed and maintained by Con Edison.
entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall develop and maintain an inventory of all chemicals and hazardous substances, subject to reporting requirements under the Community Right To Know Law...

entities.name: Risk Management Plan
entities.legal_role: A formal plan required by Environmental Laws for the facility, which the Power Authority must prepare, submit, maintain, and which Con Edison must implement upon approval.
entities.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall be responsible for: i. Preparing, submitting, and maintaining the Risk Management Plan ('RMP') for the Facility in compliance with Environmental Laws... Con Edison shall be responsible for the implementation of the RMP...

entities.name: Material Safety Data Sheets
entities.legal_role: Documents that the Power Authority must obtain and submit for Extremely Hazardous Substances and OSHA hazardous substances present at the facility in specified quantities to designated emergency response entities.
entities.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall obtain and submit Material Safety Data Sheets ('MSDS') for any Extremely Hazardous Substance ('EHS') present at the facility... to the State Emergency Response Commission ('SERC'), the Local Emergency Planning Committee ('LEPC'), and the local fire department...

entities.name: Emergency Coordinator
entities.legal_role: An individual appointed by the Power Authority to coordinate emergency response, with notification obligations to SERC and LEPC upon presence of EHS at or above threshold quantities.
entities.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall appoint an Emergency Coordinator and shall notify the SERC and the LEPC of such appointment within 60 days of an EHS being present at the facility...

entities.name: Facility Inventory Forms and 209-U Forms
entities.legal_role: Forms required to be filed by the Power Authority after the O&M Commencement Date disclosing hazardous substances, with associated fees and provision of copies to Con Edison.
entities.evidence_quote: After the O&M Commencement Date, the Power Authority shall be responsible for the filing of all Facility Inventory Forms ('FIF'), 209-U forms and any other forms relating to the disclosure of hazardous substances...

entities.name: State Emergency Response Commission
entities.legal_role: A regulatory body to which the Power Authority must submit Material Safety Data Sheets and notify of Emergency Coordinator appointments.
entities.evidence_quote: to the State Emergency Response Commission ('SERC')... The Power Authority shall appoint an Emergency Coordinator and shall notify the SERC...

entities.name: Local Emergency Planning Committee
entities.legal_role: A local regulatory body to which the Power Authority must submit Material Safety Data Sheets and notify of Emergency Coordinator appointments.
entities.evidence_quote: to the... Local Emergency Planning Committee ('LEPC')... The Power Authority shall appoint an Emergency Coordinator and shall notify the... LEPC...

entities.name: Local Fire Department
entities.legal_role: Local emergency response entity to which the Power Authority must submit Material Safety Data Sheets for hazardous substances.
entities.evidence_quote: to the... local fire department within three (3) months of their acquisition.

entities.name: O&M Commencement Date
entities.legal_role: The date marking the start of operations and maintenance responsibilities, triggering certain obligations such as filing hazardous substance disclosure forms.
entities.evidence_quote: At least thirty (30) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date... After the O&M Commencement Date, the Power Authority shall be responsible for the filing...

relationships.relationship_type: Chemical Inventory Development and Maintenance Obligation
relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison is legally required to develop and maintain the chemical inventory, ensuring compliance with Community Right To Know Law reporting requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall develop and maintain an inventory of all chemicals and hazardous substances, subject to reporting requirements under the Community Right To Know Law...

relationships.relationship_type: Notification Obligation Regarding Chemical Inventory Changes
relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison must notify the Power Authority within five business days of any addition or removal of chemicals or hazardous substances, enabling the Power Authority to maintain accurate records and compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall notify the Power Authority within five (5) business days whenever a new such chemical or hazardous substance is brought on to the facility or whenever such chemical or hazardous substance is removed...

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority is legally responsible for preparing, submitting, and maintaining the Risk Management Plan to ensure compliance with Environmental Laws.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall be responsible for: i. Preparing, submitting, and maintaining the Risk Management Plan ('RMP') for the Facility in compliance with Environmental Laws...

relationships.relationship_type: Provision of Draft RMP for Review and Comment
relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority must provide Con Edison with a draft RMP at least 30 days prior to the O&M Commencement Date, allowing Con Edison to review and approve implementation provisions, which affects Con Edison's implementation obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: At least thirty (30) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date, the Power Authority shall provide Con Edison with a draft RMP for Con Edison's review and comment.

relationships.relationship_type: RMP Implementation Obligation Subject to Approval
relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison is responsible for implementing the RMP only after having reviewed and approved the implementation sections, ensuring control over operational compliance measures.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall be responsible for the implementation of the RMP so long as Con Edison has had an opportunity to review and approve the implementation sections of the RMP.

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority must obtain and submit MSDS for EHS and OSHA hazardous substances to designated emergency response entities, ensuring regulatory compliance and emergency preparedness.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall obtain and submit Material Safety Data Sheets ('MSDS') for any Extremely Hazardous Substance ('EHS') present at the facility... to the State Emergency Response Commission ('SERC'), the Local Emergency Planning Committee ('LEPC'), and the local fire department...

relationships.relationship_type: Emergency Coordinator Appointment and Notification Obligation
relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority must appoint an Emergency Coordinator and notify SERC and LEPC within 60 days of EHS presence, ensuring designated emergency response leadership and regulatory notification.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall appoint an Emergency Coordinator and shall notify the SERC and the LEPC of such appointment within 60 days of an EHS being present at the facility...

relationships.relationship_type: Hazardous Substance Disclosure Filing and Fee Payment Obligation
relationships.legal_significance: After the O&M Commencement Date, the Power Authority is responsible for filing all required hazardous substance disclosure forms and paying associated fees, ensuring compliance with Applicable Law.
relationships.evidence_quote: After the O&M Commencement Date, the Power Authority shall be responsible for the filing of all Facility Inventory Forms ('FIF'), 209-U forms and any other forms relating to the disclosure of hazardous substances... and for the payment of all associated filing fees...

relationships.relationship_type: Provision of Copies of Filings Obligation
relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority must provide Con Edison with copies of all hazardous substance disclosure filings, ensuring transparency and enabling Con Edison to monitor compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: and shall provide: Con Edison with a copy of each such filing.

relationships.relationship_type: Provision of Chemical Inventory Information Obligation
relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison must provide the Power Authority with chemical inventory information under its custody and control in sufficient time to complete required filings, enabling the Power Authority to fulfill its filing obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall be responsible for providing Power Authority with information related to the chemical inventory under Con Edison's custody and control at the Facility in sufficient time to allow Power Authority to complete the required forms.

extractor_notes: This clause delineates detailed compliance obligations between Con Edison and the Power Authority regarding chemical inventory, risk management, emergency coordination, and hazardous substance reporting under the Community Right To Know Law and related environmental regulations. The clause explicitly assigns preparation, notification, implementation, and filing duties, with clear timelines and approval rights, reflecting a complex regulatory compliance framework.

source_clause_title: 3.1.4 In accordance with Article XIX (Indemnification), the Power Authority will indemnify

entities.name: Power Authority
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to indemnify and hold harmless Con Edison for NERC obligations and penalties during the Compliance Transfer Periods.
entities.evidence_quote: the Power Authority will indemnify and hold Con Edison harmless, to the extent allowed by law, for all such NERC obligations and penalties

entities.name: Con Edison
entities.legal_role: The party indemnified by the Power Authority during the Compliance Transfer Periods and who assumes compliance responsibility after those periods.
entities.evidence_quote: the Power Authority will indemnify and hold Con Edison harmless... After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison will assume the responsibility for managing compliance

entities.name: Compliance Transfer Periods
entities.legal_role: The defined timeframe during which the Power Authority manages compliance and indemnifies Con Edison, after which Con Edison assumes compliance responsibility.
entities.evidence_quote: hereinafter referred to as the "Compliance Transfer Periods"... After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison will assume the responsibility

entities.name: NERC Reliability Standards, NPCC Reliability Standards, NPCC Directories and NPCC Criteria Requirements
entities.legal_role: The specific regulatory standards and requirements that govern compliance and trigger indemnification and accountability obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: each individual applicable NERC Reliability Standard, NPCC Reliability Standards, NPCC Directories and NPCC Criteria Requirements

entities.name: Documentary evidence required to demonstrate compliance
entities.legal_role: The evidence that Con Edison must provide to the Power Authority to demonstrate compliance during the Compliance Transfer Periods.
entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall provide the Power Authority with documentary evidence required to demonstrate compliance

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority's obligation to indemnify and hold Con Edison harmless protects Con Edison from liabilities related to NERC obligations and penalties during the Compliance Transfer Periods.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Power Authority will indemnify and hold Con Edison harmless, to the extent allowed by law, for all such NERC obligations and penalties

relationships.legal_significance: During the Compliance Transfer Periods, the Power Authority is responsible for managing compliance and accountability for violations and penalties under the specified standards.
relationships.evidence_quote: For the individual NERC Reliability Standards... that the Power Authority has responsibility for managing compliance, maintaining documentation, and the accountability for compliance violations and penalties, during the Compliance Transfer Periods

relationships.legal_significance: After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison assumes responsibility and accountability for compliance and penalties under the applicable standards.
relationships.evidence_quote: After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison will assume the responsibility for managing compliance... and the accountability for compliance violations and penalties

relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison must provide documentary evidence to the Power Authority to demonstrate compliance during the Compliance Transfer Periods, enabling the Power Authority to fulfill its compliance management obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall provide the Power Authority with documentary evidence required to demonstrate compliance

relationships.legal_significance: The NERC and NPCC standards contain specific timeframes that define when retesting or analysis must occur, which delineates the Compliance Transfer Periods.
relationships.evidence_quote: each individual NERC Reliability Standard, NPCC Reliability Standards, NPCC Directories and NPCC Criteria Requirements contains a specific timeframe when such retesting or analysis must occur

extractor_notes: The clause defines a temporal division of compliance responsibility and indemnification between the Power Authority and Con Edison, centered on the Compliance Transfer Periods, with explicit obligations for evidence provision and accountability for penalties.

source_clause_title: 3.1.7 Management of Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) Standards:

entities.name: NYPA
entities.legal_role: NYPA is the party responsible for determining whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and for certain compliance and cost responsibilities under the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: NYPA will determine whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and determine the existence of Critical Cyber Assets.

entities.name: Astoria Annex Substation
entities.legal_role: The Astoria Annex Substation is the facility subject to determination as a Critical Asset and Critical Cyber Assets, triggering compliance obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: NYPA will determine whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and determine the existence of Critical Cyber Assets.

entities.name: Critical Asset
entities.legal_role: A designation that triggers mandatory compliance with CIP-003 through CIP-009 standards and related responsibilities.
entities.evidence_quote: If Power Authority determines that the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset with Critical Cyber Assets, then compliance required by CIP-003 through CIP-009 shall be applied

entities.name: Critical Cyber Assets
entities.legal_role: Cyber assets whose designation as critical triggers specific compliance and operational change obligations under CIP standards.
entities.evidence_quote: If Power Authority determines that the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset with Critical Cyber Assets, then compliance required by CIP-003 through CIP-009 shall be applied

entities.name: CIP-003 through CIP-009
entities.legal_role: A set of mandatory cybersecurity standards that must be complied with if the facility is designated as a Critical Asset with Critical Cyber Assets.
entities.evidence_quote: compliance required by CIP-003 through CIP-009 shall be applied

entities.name: Power Authority's sole cost and expense
entities.legal_role: The obligation of the Power Authority to bear all costs related to required design and operational changes for CIP compliance.
entities.evidence_quote: any required changes to the existing design and operation of the Facility shall be the Power Authority's sole responsibility and performed at the Power Authority's sole cost and expense

entities.name: O&M Commencement Date
entities.legal_role: The date before which certain compliance and operational changes must be completed and after which operational responsibilities transfer.
entities.evidence_quote: Prior to the O&M Commencement Date... must be completed at least sixty (60) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date

entities.name: Con Edison
entities.legal_role: The party responsible for adhering to policies, performing functional activities related to NERC CIP compliance, and managing compliance audits after certain periods.
entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall be required to adhere to Con Edison's policies and procedures, and Con Edison shall perform all functional activities related to NERC CIP compliance

entities.name: Compliance Transfer Periods
entities.legal_role: The period after which Con Edison assumes responsibility for managing protections and compliance evidence.
entities.evidence_quote: After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison will assume responsibility for managing those protections, maintaining the required compliance evidence

entities.name: NYPA as registered Transmission Owner
entities.legal_role: NYPA's role as the registered Transmission Owner includes retaining responsibility for determining Critical Asset status and existence of Critical Cyber Assets.
entities.evidence_quote: NYPA, as the registered Transmission Owner for the Astoria Annex Substation, will retain responsibility for the determination of whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset

relationships.legal_significance: NYPA's determination of whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset triggers compliance obligations under CIP standards.
relationships.evidence_quote: NYPA will determine whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset

relationships.relationship_type: Determination of Existence of Critical Cyber Assets
relationships.legal_significance: NYPA's determination of Critical Cyber Assets existence triggers application of CIP compliance requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: NYPA will determine... the existence of Critical Cyber Assets

relationships.legal_significance: Designation as a Critical Asset with Critical Cyber Assets mandates compliance with CIP-003 through CIP-009 standards.
relationships.evidence_quote: If Power Authority determines that the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset with Critical Cyber Assets, then compliance required by CIP-003 through CIP-009 shall be applied

relationships.legal_significance: Power Authority bears sole cost and expense for design and operational changes required for CIP compliance if the facility is a Critical Asset.
relationships.evidence_quote: any required changes to the existing design and operation of the Facility shall be the Power Authority's sole responsibility and performed at the Power Authority's sole cost and expense

relationships.relationship_type: Deadline for Completion of Compliance Changes
relationships.legal_significance: All design and operational changes required for CIP compliance must be completed at least 60 days prior to the O&M Commencement Date.
relationships.evidence_quote: all such design and operational changes required to comply with the obligations of being a Critical Cyber Asset must be completed at least sixty (60) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date

relationships.relationship_type: Performance of Functional Activities for CIP Compliance
relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison is obligated to perform all functional activities related to NERC CIP compliance and provide documentary evidence to NYPA.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall perform all functional activities related to NERC CIP compliance and shall provide the Power Authority with documentary evidence required to demonstrate compliance

relationships.legal_significance: After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison assumes responsibility for managing protections, compliance evidence, and audits.
relationships.evidence_quote: After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison will assume responsibility for managing those protections, maintaining the required compliance evidence, and managing compliance audits

relationships.legal_significance: NYPA retains responsibility for determining Critical Asset and Critical Cyber Asset status as the registered Transmission Owner.
relationships.evidence_quote: NYPA, as the registered Transmission Owner for the Astoria Annex Substation, will retain responsibility for the determination of whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and determining the existence of Critical Cyber Assets

relationships.relationship_type: Delegation of Compliance Establishment and Management
relationships.legal_significance: If protections are not necessary until after the O&M Commencement Date, NYPA directs Con Edison to establish and manage CIP protections at NYPA's sole cost and expense.
relationships.evidence_quote: at Power Authority's direction, Con Edison shall be responsible at Power Authority's sole cost and expense, for establishing and managing the protections required by CIP-002 through CIP-009

extractor_notes: The clause clearly delineates responsibilities and cost obligations between NYPA (Power Authority) and Con Edison regarding CIP compliance, with specific timing requirements tied to the O&M Commencement Date and Compliance Transfer Periods. The role of NYPA as registered Transmission Owner is emphasized for determinations of criticality, while Con Edison is assigned operational compliance tasks post-transfer.

entities.name: The Parties
entities.legal_role: The Parties are the contractual entities bound by the reporting and indemnity obligations set forth in this clause.
entities.evidence_quote: The Parties agree that in the event that either Party discovers on or about the Easement Area ... the discovering Party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide relevant information ...

entities.name: Easement Area
entities.legal_role: The Easement Area is the geographic location subject to reporting obligations and potential environmental incidents under the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: on or about the Easement Area (as defined in the Easement Grant, as may be amended)

entities.name: Facility
entities.legal_role: The Facility is a defined location subject to environmental condition discovery and reporting obligations under the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: on or about the Easement Area ... or the Facility any condition or incident reasonably believed to trigger any of the reporting obligations

entities.name: Discovering Party
entities.legal_role: The Discovering Party is the Party who identifies a condition or incident triggering reporting obligations and must notify the other Party.
entities.evidence_quote: the discovering Party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide relevant information as quickly as practicable to the other Party

entities.name: Power Authority
entities.legal_role: The Power Authority is the Party responsible for reporting Releases to Governmental Authorities and indemnifying Con Edison for penalties arising from failure to report.
entities.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall report to the proper Governmental Authorities any reportable Releases ... the Power Authority shall indemnify Con Edison for any such assessed penalties

entities.name: Con Edison
entities.legal_role: Con Edison is the Party that may discover Releases, provide information to the Power Authority, and may report Releases if the Power Authority fails to do so timely.
entities.evidence_quote: provided that if Con Edison is the discovering party, it provides the Power Authority with relevant information ... Con Edison may report such Release

entities.name: Governmental Authorities
entities.legal_role: Governmental Authorities are the regulatory bodies to whom reportable Releases must be reported under applicable laws.
entities.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall report to the proper Governmental Authorities any reportable Releases within the time required by applicable federal, state, local, and Environmental Laws

entities.name: Reportable Release
entities.legal_role: A Reportable Release is an environmental release incident triggering mandatory reporting obligations under applicable laws and this contract.
entities.evidence_quote: any reportable Releases within the time required by applicable federal, state, local, and Environmental Laws

entities.name: Reporting Obligations under Section 12.1(b)
entities.legal_role: The Reporting Obligations define the duty to notify Governmental Authorities and the other Party about reportable Releases within legally mandated timeframes.
entities.evidence_quote: any condition or incident reasonably believed to trigger any of the reporting obligations set forth in Section 12.1 (b)

entities.name: Indemnity Obligation of Power Authority to Con Edison
entities.legal_role: The Indemnity Obligation requires the Power Authority to compensate Con Edison for penalties assessed due to the Power Authority's failure to timely report Releases.
entities.evidence_quote: the Power Authority shall indemnify Con Edison for any such assessed penalties pursuant to Section 12.5

entities.entity_type: Spill Prevention, Control and Countermeasure Plan
entities.name: Spill Prevention, Control and Countermeasure Plan
entities.legal_role: The Plan governs the actions Con Edison must take to control and contain Releases consistent with applicable laws and requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: consistent with applicable federal, state, local, and Environmental Laws, or Power Authority requirements and the Spill Prevention, Control and Countermeasure Plan

entities.name: Remediation Obligations under Section 12.2
entities.legal_role: The Remediation Obligations define the Parties' rights and duties regarding cleanup activities, which are not altered by Section 12.1(c).
entities.evidence_quote: This Section 12.1 (c) does not change any rights or obligations of the Parties with respect to Remediation under Section 12.2

relationships.legal_significance: The discovering Party must notify the other Party promptly to ensure timely awareness and compliance with reporting obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: the discovering Party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide relevant information as quickly as practicable to the other Party

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority is legally obligated to report reportable Releases to Governmental Authorities within required timeframes to comply with environmental laws.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall report to the proper Governmental Authorities any reportable Releases within the time required by applicable federal, state, local, and Environmental Laws

relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison must provide the Power Authority with relevant information about Releases to enable timely reporting to Governmental Authorities.
relationships.evidence_quote: if Con Edison is the discovering party, it provides the Power Authority with relevant information regarding such Release within sufficient time

relationships.legal_significance: If the Power Authority fails to report timely, Con Edison may report the Release to Governmental Authorities to ensure compliance with legal requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison may report such Release

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority must indemnify Con Edison for penalties assessed due to the Power Authority's failure to report Releases timely, protecting Con Edison from financial harm.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Power Authority shall indemnify Con Edison for any such assessed penalties pursuant to Section 12.5

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Control and Contain Releases
relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison must take actions consistent with the Spill Prevention Plan and applicable laws to control and contain Releases, ensuring environmental and safety compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall promptly take all actions reasonably necessary to control and contain the release ... consistent with ... the Spill Prevention, Control and Countermeasure Plan

relationships.legal_significance: Section 12.1(c) explicitly states it does not alter the Parties' rights or obligations regarding Remediation under Section 12.2, preserving existing remediation duties.
relationships.evidence_quote: This Section 12.1 (c) does not change any rights or obligations of the Parties with respect to Remediation under Section 12.2

extractor_notes: The clause establishes a detailed framework for environmental release reporting, including notification duties between the Parties, reporting to Governmental Authorities, indemnity for failure to report, and control and containment obligations. The interplay between Con Edison and the Power Authority is central, with clear allocation of reporting responsibilities and indemnity. The clause also clarifies that remediation obligations under a separate section remain unaffected.

source_clause_title: 17.2 Right to Terminate. If a Breach is not cured as provided in this Article XVII, or if a Breach is

entities.legal_role: The contract subject to termination upon Default declared by the non-Breaching Party.

entities.name: Default
entities.legal_role: A legal status declared by the non-Breaching Party upon uncured Breach, triggering termination rights.
entities.evidence_quote: the non-Breaching Party shall thereafter have the right to declare a Default

entities.name: Breach

entities.evidence_quote: If a Breach is not cured as provided in this Article XVII, or if a Breach is not capable of being cured

relationships.legal_significance: The non-Breaching Party has the legal right to declare Default if the Breach is not cured, enabling termination.

relationships.evidence_quote: the non-Breaching Party shall thereafter have the right to declare a Default and terminate this Second Composite Agreement

entities.name: spill kits and other emergency response equipment

entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall maintain spill kits and other emergency response equipment, if necessary, at the Astoria Annex Substation.

entities.name: Release of hazardous waste

entities.evidence_quote: In the event of a Release of hazardous waste, Con Edison shall take immediate action to contain and control the Release

relationships.relationship_type: Hazardous Waste Emergency Response Notification Obligation

relationships.relationship_type: Hazardous Waste Emergency Preparedness Maintenance Obligation

relationships.legal_significance: Upon a hazardous waste release, Con Edison must take immediate action to contain and control the release, demonstrating a duty to mitigate environmental harm.
relationships.evidence_quote: In the event of a Release of hazardous waste, Con Edison shall take immediate action to contain and control the Release

entities.name: The Power Authority
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Con Edison Indemnified Parties against Environmental Liabilities.
entities.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Con Edison, its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, shareholders, and employees

entities.name: Con Edison Indemnified Parties
entities.legal_role: The parties entitled to indemnification, defense, and hold harmless protection from the Power Authority for Environmental Liabilities.
entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison, its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, shareholders, and employees (collectively, the "Con Edison Indemnified Parties")

entities.name: Environmental Liabilities
entities.legal_role: The subject matter of indemnification, including liabilities arising from Releases, Remediation, Additional Work, Risks, and waste transportation and disposal related to the Easement Area and Con Edison Lands.
entities.evidence_quote: any and all Environmental Liabilities arising out of or in connection with (i) the existence of or any actual or threatened Release or Discovery ... (iii) Required Remediation; (iv) the Additional Work, (v) the Risks, and (vi) the transportation and disposal of any and all wastes

entities.entity_type: Real Property Interest Subject to Environmental Conditions
entities.name: Easement Area
entities.legal_role: The geographic area where Releases, Discoveries, and environmental conditions triggering indemnification occur.
entities.evidence_quote: the existence of or any actual or threatened Release or Discovery on, over, above, under or around the Easement Area

entities.entity_type: Real Property Interest Subject to Environmental Conditions
entities.name: Con Edison Lands
entities.legal_role: The lands through which Hazardous Substances may migrate and where wastes subject to Required Remediation may be located, triggering indemnification obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: any migration of such Hazardous Substances on, to or through the Con Edison Lands; ... wastes subject to Required Remediation on the Con Edison Lands

entities.name: Associated Equipment
entities.legal_role: Equipment owned by the Power Authority from the date of ownership forward, relevant to the scope of indemnification for environmental liabilities.
entities.evidence_quote: that occurs from the date of the Power Authority ownership of the Associated Equipment forward

entities.name: Release
entities.legal_role: An actual or threatened discharge or escape of Hazardous Substances triggering indemnification obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: any actual or threatened Release or Discovery on, over, above, under or around the Easement Area

entities.name: Required Remediation
entities.legal_role: Environmental remediation activities mandated to address contamination, triggering indemnification.
entities.evidence_quote: Required Remediation

entities.name: Additional Work
entities.legal_role: Supplemental environmental work related to the Easement Area or Con Edison Lands that triggers indemnification.
entities.evidence_quote: the Additional Work

entities.name: Risks
entities.legal_role: Environmental risks associated with the Easement Area and Con Edison Lands that are subject to indemnification.
entities.evidence_quote: the Risks

entities.name: Transportation and disposal of wastes
entities.legal_role: The handling of wastes from the Facility and Easement Area, including those subject to remediation, which triggers indemnification obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: the transportation and disposal of any and all wastes from the Facility and the Easement Area, as well as any and all wastes subject to Required Remediation on the Con Edison Lands

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority is legally obligated to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Con Edison Indemnified Parties against Environmental Liabilities, protecting them from losses and claims.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Power Authority shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Con Edison, its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, shareholders, and employees

relationships.legal_significance: Environmental Liabilities are the specific liabilities for which the Con Edison Indemnified Parties are protected under the indemnity obligation.
relationships.evidence_quote: for any and all Environmental Liabilities arising out of or in connection with ...

relationships.legal_significance: Environmental Liabilities arise from conditions and events occurring on or around the Easement Area, defining the spatial scope of indemnification.
relationships.evidence_quote: arising out of or in connection with (i) the existence of or any actual or threatened Release or Discovery on, over, above, under or around the Easement Area

relationships.legal_significance: Environmental Liabilities also arise from migration of Hazardous Substances and wastes on or through the Con Edison Lands, expanding the indemnification scope.
relationships.evidence_quote: any migration of such Hazardous Substances on, to or through the Con Edison Lands; ... wastes subject to Required Remediation on the Con Edison Lands

relationships.legal_significance: The occurrence or threat of a Release triggers the indemnification obligation for Environmental Liabilities.
relationships.evidence_quote: any actual or threatened Release or Discovery

relationships.legal_significance: Required Remediation activities are included within the scope of Environmental Liabilities indemnified by the Power Authority.
relationships.evidence_quote: Required Remediation

relationships.legal_significance: Additional Work related to environmental conditions is included within the scope of indemnified Environmental Liabilities.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Additional Work

relationships.legal_significance: Environmental Risks are part of the liabilities for which indemnification is provided.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Risks

relationships.legal_significance: Transportation and disposal of wastes from the Facility and Easement Area are activities that give rise to indemnifiable Environmental Liabilities.
relationships.evidence_quote: the transportation and disposal of any and all wastes from the Facility and the Easement Area

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority's ownership of the Associated Equipment from a certain date forward defines the temporal scope of indemnification obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: from the date of the Power Authority ownership of the Associated Equipment forward

extractor_notes: The clause defines a broad indemnity by the Power Authority to Con Edison and related parties for environmental liabilities connected to specific geographic areas and activities, including Releases, Remediation, and waste handling. The clause explicitly identifies the indemnified parties, the indemnitor, the scope of liabilities, and the temporal scope linked to ownership of equipment.

source_clause_title: 34.1 No delay or omission by either party to exercise any right or power accruing upon a non­

entities.name: Consolidated Edison Company of New York, Inc.
entities.legal_role: This entity is a party to the Second Composite Agreement and is responsible for performing obligations under the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: CONSOLIDATED EDISON COMPANY OF NEW YORK, INC.

entities.name: New York Power Authority
entities.legal_role: This entity is a party to the Second Composite Agreement and holds rights and obligations under the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: NEW YORK POWER AUTHORITY

entities.name: Second Composite Agreement
entities.legal_role: This is the overarching agreement executed by the parties that governs their rights and obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: the parties have executed this Second Gomposite Agreement

entities.name: Sulfur Hexafluoride (SF6) gas
entities.legal_role: This substance is subject to tracking and reporting requirements under the contract due to environmental regulations.
entities.evidence_quote: The Astoria Annex Substation will contain Sulfur Hexafluoride (SF6) gas.

entities.name: Astoria Annex Substation
entities.legal_role: This facility is the location where O&M services are provided and where SF6 gas tracking and reporting obligations apply.
entities.evidence_quote: the Astoria Annex Substation shall be generally kept unstaffed and Con Edison shall provide any staffing

entities.name: O&M Services
entities.legal_role: These are the operations and maintenance services that Con Edison is obligated to provide at the Astoria Annex Substation.
entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall provide any staffing at the Astoria Annex Substation for its O&M Services

entities.name: Tracking of SF6 Gas Inventory, Additions and Losses
entities.legal_role: This duty requires Con Edison to track SF6 gas usage and emissions in Power Authority equipment, reflecting environmental compliance obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: Con Edison will be responsible for tracking the inventory, additions and losses of SF6 gas in the Power Authority equipment

entities.name: Power Authority's commitment to the Climate Registry
entities.legal_role: This commitment triggers the need for SF6 gas tracking and reporting under the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: Due to the Power Authority's commitment to the Climate Registry

entities.name: Environmental Protection Agency's proposed mandatory gas reporting rule
entities.legal_role: This anticipated regulation motivates the contractual obligation to track SF6 gas emissions.
entities.evidence_quote: in anticipation of the Environmental Protection Agency's proposed mandatory gas reporting rule

entities.name: Waiver of Covenants, Conditions or Agreements
entities.legal_role: This provision clarifies that delay or omission in exercising rights does not constitute waiver of those rights or future breaches.
entities.evidence_quote: A waiver by either party of any of the covenants, conditions or agreements hereof to be performed by the other party shall not be construed to be a waiver of any subsequent breach hereof

relationships.legal_significance: Consolidated Edison is legally bound by the terms and obligations set forth in the Second Composite Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: the parties have executed this Second Gomposite Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: New York Power Authority is legally bound by the terms and obligations set forth in the Second Composite Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: the parties have executed this Second Gomposite Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison is obligated to provide staffing and O&M services at the Astoria Annex Substation under the contract.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison shall provide any staffing at the Astoria Annex Substation for its O&M Services

relationships.legal_significance: Con Edison is contractually responsible for tracking SF6 gas inventory and emissions, ensuring compliance with environmental commitments.
relationships.evidence_quote: Con Edison will be responsible for tracking the inventory, additions and losses of SF6 gas

relationships.legal_significance: The tracking obligation specifically concerns the SF6 gas contained in Power Authority equipment, which is regulated due to environmental concerns.
relationships.evidence_quote: tracking the inventory, additions and losses of SF6 gas in the Power Authority equipment

relationships.legal_significance: The Power Authority's environmental commitment triggers the contractual requirement for SF6 gas tracking by Con Edison.
relationships.evidence_quote: Due to the Power Authority's commitment to the Climate Registry... Accordingly, Con Edison will be responsible for tracking

relationships.legal_significance: The anticipated EPA gas reporting rule motivates the inclusion of SF6 gas tracking obligations in the contract.
relationships.evidence_quote: in anticipation of the Environmental Protection Agency's proposed mandatory gas reporting rule

relationships.legal_significance: This clause clarifies that failure or delay to exercise rights does not waive those rights or future breaches, preserving parties' legal protections.
relationships.evidence_quote: A waiver by either party of any of the covenants, conditions or agreements hereof to be performed by the other party shall not be construed to be a waiver of any subsequent breach hereof

extractor_notes: The clause includes a waiver provision clarifying non-waiver of rights despite delay or omission, and detailed environmental compliance obligations related to SF6 gas tracking at the Astoria Annex Substation, with Con Edison as the obligated party and the Power Authority as the environmental commitment holder. The clause also contains execution details of the Second Composite Agreement by authorized representatives.
